programs.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programs.desc: Most people know it as the biblical birthplace of man -- the Garden of Eden; investigating if the Garden of Eden was just a story to explain human creation or if it was a real place.

programs.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programs.sub_title: The Amazing Events of the Book of Exodus
programs.desc: The Burning Bush, the 10 Plagues, the Parting of the Red Sea these Biblical events in the Book of Exodus have shaped Judeo-Christian beliefs for centuries.

programs.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programs.desc: The Valentine's Day Massacre, a shocking event in Chicago's history, occurred on February 14th, 1929; it's an unsolved mystery.

programs.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programs.desc: John Dillinger's life consists of brazen bank robberies across the country, but his death remains shrouded in mystery; what really happened to John Dillinger?

programs.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programs.desc: Notorious mobster Bugsy Siegel is gunned down in his Beverly Hills living room; Siegel's killing becomes front page news; no arrest is ever made.

programs.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programs.desc: In a perfectly planned robbery, thieves steal half a billion dollars in masterpieces from the Gardner Museum in Boston; it's been three decades since the brazen heist, and an exhaustive search has failed to recover the stolen goods.
